reasoning: The baseline date of Oct 26 typically has a mean temperature around 15-18°C. Subsequent resolution dates (Nov 2, Nov 25, Jan 24) fall progressively deeper into winter, when historical mean temperatures in Marignane drop significantly (e.g., to ~10°C in late Nov, ~7.5°C in Jan). Physically, the actual probability of these dates being uniquely warmer than an Oct 26 baseline steadily decreases (moving further from 0.5). However, strictly applying the meta-instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5", the probabilities have been constrained such that their distance to 0.5 strictly decreases to reflect higher theoretical epistemic uncertainty over time. Using an anchor of 0.20 for the 7-day forecast (which leans confidently downward), the 30-day and 90-day forecasts are drawn progressively toward 0.5.
